name: Checkmk REST API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade IT infrastructure monitoring platform with REST API for managing hosts, services, downtimes, automations, and metric collection across large on-premises and hybrid environments.
use_cases: Dynamic host management — adding/removing monitored hosts as infrastructure changes
Scheduling maintenance windows and downtimes from deployment automation agents
Querying service state and performance data for automated incident correlation
Executing bulk configuration changes across large host sets
Extracting SLA metrics and availability data for reporting agents
not_for: Cloud-native Kubernetes monitoring without Kubernetes monitoring integration
Application-level tracing and distributed systems debugging
Organizations without ops staff to configure Checkmk rules and check plugins
best_when: An agent manages IT operations for enterprises running Checkmk over thousands of servers, network devices, and applications.
avoid_when: You're building modern cloud-native observability — Prometheus or Grafana Cloud are better fits.

auth.notes: Automation user tokens created in Checkmk web interface. Bearer token authentication for API requests. User-level permissions inherited from Checkmk user role system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Checkmk Raw Edition (CRE): fully open-source, unlimited hosts
pricing.paid_tiers: Checkmk Cloud: from €0.005/host/hour; Checkmk Enterprise from €600/year
pricing.notes: Raw Edition is a fully functional open-source version. Enterprise Edition adds automated configuration (Bakery), dashboards, and support. Good balance of open-source and commercial.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: determined by server capacity; typically no explicit limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Checkmk 2.x REST API has excellent documentation with OpenAPI-style descriptions and examples. Error responses are well-structured JSON. API is significantly improved over v1 WATO API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Host creation and modification are idempotent with consistent naming. Configuration activation (activate_changes) required after most write operations — agents must include this step.
known_agent_gotchas: Changes to configuration MUST be activated via activate_changes endpoint — changes are staged, not immediate
Activation requires specifying affected sites in distributed monitoring environments
Service discovery must be run separately after adding hosts before services appear
API operations on sites work differently in distributed (clustered) Checkmk setups

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable (required in production). Bearer token authentication. Open-source codebase is auditable. User permissions inherited from Checkmk RBAC. Open-source edition allows full security audit.
